perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or documents of which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be promptly returned, accompanied by copies of such documentation, after the evaluation set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or documents which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be returned after the evaluation set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'promptly returned, accompanied by copies of such documentation,' there's no longer a clear requirement to return *copies* of the documentation. This creates an in-text contradiction, making the requirements of return incomplete and opening to interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or documents which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be returned after the evaluation set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5 explicitly requires both the return of materials and documents *along with copies* of such documentation. The changed text removes 'accompanied by copies of such documentation,' thus eliminating the explicit requirement to return copies. Given that the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, the omission directly contradicts the original intention of returning copies, creating a strong in-text contradiction as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Remedies. Recipient agrees that its obliga tions hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. A ccordingly, Recipient agrees an d acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, th e Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Remedies. Recipient agrees that its obliga tions hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covenants and agreements set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the second half of the paragraph: 'Accordingly, Recipient agrees an d acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, th e Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.' the Discloser loses the explicit right to injunctive relief. This directly contradicts the initial statement about the obligations being necessary and reasonable to protect the business, as the remedies are now potentially inadequate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Remedies. Recipient agrees that its obliga tions hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covenants and agreements set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Discloser's explicit entitlement to injunctive relief for breaches, contradicting the original intent of ensuring obligations are necessary and reasonable to protect the business. The contradicted text, which is the same as the original text, expresses the need for adequate remedies, and the removal of the injunctive relief clause directly undermines this, creating a strong contradiction.
